originalAlarm.description: Value stored to '__codec_hw_write_7' is never read

sanitizedDescription: Value stored to 'codec_hw_write' is never read
presenceCondition:
(and (or (and (not CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642) CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642)
         CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642)
     (or (and (not CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642) CONFIG_I2C CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642)
         (and CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 CONFIG_I2C)))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_I2C () Bool
  true)
reasoning: 66517915e09.c L24: Dead store of `codec_hw_write` in ak4642_init. `int codec_hw_write = i2c_master_send;` at L24 — codec_hw_write is assigned but never read (function returns 0 immediately). Not marked // ERROR (the ERROR comments refer to the I2C=F non-compilable case). New bug. PC simplifies to CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642=T AND CONFIG_I2C=T (no library macros remain after simplification). FSI_AK4642=T implies SOC_AK4642=T (derived macro). Real condition: SOC_AK4642=T (so ak4642_init is compiled) AND I2C=T (so i2c_master_send is defined). PC correctly constrained.
